description:
You are a storage-systems administrator who has just been told that the company’s backup appliance will start exporting data over two network-storage protocols: NFS (TCP 2049) and iSCSI (TCP 3260).  
 Before the appliance is connected, you have to prepare a tiny “change bundle” that the datacenter firewall team will pick up and run as soon as the maintenance window opens.

 What you must deliver
 ---------------------
 1. A directory called /home/user/firewall_logs  
 2. Inside that directory, a plain-text file named firewall_commands.log that contains the **three** exact firewall commands the team must run.  
 3. In your home directory, an executable bash script named /home/user/firewall_setup.sh that contains the same three commands and starts with a proper shebang.

 Exact text that must appear in firewall_commands.log
 ----------------------------------------------------
 The file must have **exactly** these three lines in this exact order, with no leading/trailing spaces and each line terminated by a UNIX newline (LF):

 ufw allow 2049/tcp comment 'Allow NFS service'
 ufw allow 3260/tcp comment 'Allow iSCSI service'
 ufw reload

 The automated test will fail if:
 • There is any deviation in spelling, capitalisation, spacing, quoting or line-order.  
 • A blank line is present before, between or after the three lines.  
 • The file contains more or fewer than three lines.

 Requirements for /home/user/firewall_setup.sh
 ---------------------------------------------
 • Must be executable (chmod 755).  
 • First line must be the shebang: #!/bin/bash  
 • The next three lines must be the **same** three ufw commands shown above, in the same order.  
 • No additional commands, comments or blank lines are allowed.

 Summary checklist
 -----------------
 • Create /home/user/firewall_logs (if it does not exist).  
 • Place firewall_commands.log inside it with the three lines shown above.  
 • Create /home/user/firewall_setup.sh at the top of your home directory with the shebang plus those three commands.  
 • Ensure /home/user/firewall_setup.sh is executable.

 The whole job can be done with only three-to-four shell commands plus chmod.
truth:
Directory: /home/user/firewall_logs  (permissions 755)

 File: /home/user/firewall_logs/firewall_commands.log
 Exact content (including final newline):
 ufw allow 2049/tcp comment 'Allow NFS service'\n
 ufw allow 3260/tcp comment 'Allow iSCSI service'\n
 ufw reload\n

 File: /home/user/firewall_setup.sh  (permissions 755)
 Exact content (including final newline):
 #!/bin/bash\n
 ufw allow 2049/tcp comment 'Allow NFS service'\n
 ufw allow 3260/tcp comment 'Allow iSCSI service'\n
 ufw reload\n
name: task_000000_2b67854f
